textPlain: I've been using bat for several years and it's been a really nice drop-in replacement for cat/less/etc.Like rg, it's one of those "rewrite it in rust" projects that turned out to actually be quite well thought through.
Other threads about (IMHO very nice) tools by this developer (fd, hexyl, and hyperfine),https://news.ycombinator.com/from?site=github.com/sharkdp
cat <whatever> | highlight --force -O xterm256 | less -SRNIworks fine for me.
and rsync works fine for people too, I guess that's why nobody uses Dropbox

I call cat when I need to pipe or copy something, and just bat if I wanna read it myself. I find this as a good compromise, I like how bat formats things.Your solution would be ok with an alias as well, so thanks. Might try it just so I dont need yet another program lying around
